housePricePremium.note: Average house price premium (£ thousands) within catchment areas of top-rated state schools vs surrounding neighbourhood. England.

fsmRatio.note: Ratio of FSM-eligible pupils at oversubscribed outstanding/good schools vs local area FSM average. A ratio of 0.60 means these schools have 40% fewer FSM pupils than their locality.

admissionsAppeals.note: School admissions appeals upheld in favour of parents, England. Includes all state school types.

sources.name: Nationwide / Lloyds Banking Group
sources.dataset: School catchment area house price analysis

sources.name: Department for Education
sources.dataset: School characteristics data — FSM eligibility by school

metadata.methodology: House price premium calculated by comparing median sold prices within 200m of rated Outstanding/Good schools vs median for the surrounding LSOA. FSM ratio compares proportion of FSM-eligible pupils on roll at oversubscribed schools vs FSM rate in the school's local authority. Appeals data from DfE statutory returns.
knownIssues: House price premium analysis does not control for other neighbourhood characteristics — schools and premium may both reflect underlying area quality
FSM ratio data affected by free school meal universal infant expansion which changed FSM take-up patterns
School admissions appeals data understates total disputes as many families do not appeal formally
